From CRO Chaos
to Benchling Clarity

An AI-powered data harmonization platform that transforms fragmented CRO datasets into structured, validated, Benchling-ready scientific records — in minutes, not weeks.

Scroll
The Challenge

CRO Data is a Bottleneck

Every CRO delivers data differently. Different formats, schemas, naming conventions, file types. Scientists waste days manually harmonizing data before any analysis can begin.

📂
Heterogeneous Data Formats
CROs deliver XLSX, CSV, JSON, TSV — each with their own column names, structures, and conventions. No two datasets look the same.
⏳
Manual Harmonization Takes Weeks
Scientists spend 60–80% of data processing time on manual column mapping, format cleaning, and schema alignment before they can run a single query.
🔗
No Direct Benchling Integration
CRO data rarely conforms to Benchling's schema structure. Every import requires bespoke mapping files, spreadsheet wrangling, and IT intervention.
⚠️
Data Quality Risks
Manual processes introduce transcription errors, missed validations, unit mismatches, and duplicate barcodes — compromising data integrity.
0
% of time lost to manual data prep
0
CRO file formats per study on average
0
schema fields per harmonized dataset
Real-World Scenario

Monday Morning.
Six CRO Files. One Scientist.

Meet Dr. Priya — a Senior Research Scientist at a global biopharmaceutical company. It's 9 AM. Her ADC oncology study just received data back from three different CROs. Here's exactly what lands in her inbox.

👩‍🔬
Dr. Priya Sharma · Senior Research Scientist, Global Biopharma
📥 Inbox — Monday 9:04 AM — 6 new attachments from 3 CROs
INBOX
Search mail…
CRO-Alpha · 9:04 AM
ADC Compound Screening Results
Hi Priya, attached our compound bioactivity data. Please find 2 files enclosed.
📊 Compound Data - CRO 2.xlsx 📄 Compound Data - CRO 1.json
CRO-Beta · 8:51 AM
Batch Storage + QC Data
Attached batch storage records and QC results for ADC samples 1–20.
📊 Batch & storage.xlsx 📊 Sample QC Data.xlsx
CRO-Gamma · 8:22 AM
Instrument Results + Sequences
Please find our instrument assay results and sequence readouts for the ADC panel.
📊 Instruments & Results.xlsx 📊 Sequence Data.xlsx
💬 Dr. Priya: "Great, 6 files. Let me open them and see what we're dealing with…"
😰 She opens the files. Here's what she actually sees.
📊 Compound Data - CRO 2.xlsx
📄 Compound Data - CRO 1.json
📊 Batch & Storage.xlsx
📊 Sample QC Data.xlsx
📊 Sequence Data.xlsx
⚠ 12 rows · 14 columns · Compound Data - CRO 2.xlsx 4 issues detected ↓
RESULT_TAG ? ID CID ? COMPOUND_NAME DATASOURCE_SMILES ? mol_wt ? Hydrophobicity giprct ? unit ?
RESULT_TYPE—————FLOATINTEGER—
RESULT_DESCR—————concentration tested…Inhibition of growth…—
2024-01-12570102AM_3875946(2S)-2-[[(2-amino…CCC(C)CC(NC(=O)…7123.12-295g
2024-01-12562226AM_24194250N-[4-methyl-2-oxo…CC1=CC(=O)NC(=O)…7131.89-203g
⚠ Rows 1–3 are metadata headers, not data ⚠ CID = "AM_3875946" — CRO-specific format, no schema match ⚠ column "giprct" — undocumented abbreviation ⚠ unit column mixes "g" and "nm" — incompatible
⚠ Malformed JSON — Compound Data - CRO 1.json Syntax errors prevent parsing ↓
{
  "vendor": "Sigma",
  "range" : >3.76  ✗ invalid JSON — bare value with operator
  "compounds": [
    {
      "id": "PFI_7425",
      "mol_wt": 700,
      "unit": g  ✗ missing quotes — not a string
      "giprct" : 67.5  ✗ missing comma after previous line
      "giprct" : 634.1  ✗ value 634.1 — is this % inhibition or raw signal?
      "hydrophobicity": 91.34  ✗ outlier: logP of 91.34 is physically impossible
    }
  ]
}
✗ JSON.parse() fails — file cannot be loaded as-is ✗ "unit": g — bare unquoted value (syntax error) ✗ Missing commas between fields ✗ hydrophobicity = 91.34 — physically impossible outlier
⚠ 23 rows · 13 columns · Batch & Storage.xlsx 6 issues detected ↓
Batch ? Sample_Code ? sample name ? Exp_Date Purity(%) ? Storage Location ? Concentration (nm) ? Mfg_Date ?
B1ADC001ADC-Sample-12026-01-109,6-80CFZ02 × 10⁹jan 10 24
B2ADC002ADC-Sample-22026-01-1096.1-80CFZ-12 × 10⁹2024-01-10
B003ADC003ADC-Sample-32026-01-1096.2-80CFZ22 × 10⁹2024-01-10
B004ADC004ADC-Sample-42026-01-1096.3-80CFZ-32 × 10⁹2024-01-10
B005ADC005ADC-Sample-52026-01-1096.4-80CFZ-42 × 10⁹jan 10 24
⚠ Batch ID inconsistent: "B1", "B2", "B003", "B004" ⚠ Purity "9,6" — European comma decimal ⚠ Concentration "2 × 10⁹" — superscript, not numeric ⚠ Mfg_Date "jan 10 24" — non-ISO format ⚠ Location format: FZ0, FZ-1, FZ2, FZ-3 — inconsistent ⚠ Column "Sample_Code" ≠ "Sample_ID" in template
⚠ 25 rows · 10 columns · Sample QC Data.xlsx — different naming from everything else
Sample_Name Program_Type ? Analyst Notes Vector DAR_Value ? Target_Protein ? Agent ? Linker ? QC
ADC-Sample-1OncologyBJonesCalibration standardpcDNA3.13.7HER2CHOCLVPass
ADC-Sample-2OncologyALeeCalibration standardpCMV3.9EGFRHEK293NCLVPass
ADC-Sample-3OncologyALeeCalibration standardpET28a3.5HER2E coliCLVPass
⚠ "Program_Type" ≠ "Program" in your schema ⚠ "DAR_Value" ≠ "DAR" in your schema ⚠ "Target_Protein" ≠ "Target" in your schema ⚠ "Agent" — is this Host_System, Conjugation_Method, or Supplier?
⚠ 25 rows · 5 columns · Sequence Data.xlsx — raw FASTQ-style format
header ? host system ? identifier ? quality
@PFI_7425: pcDNA3.1: ATGGCTAGTACGTTAGCTAGCTA : 51.6 : 31 : CHOmAb-Construct+FFFFFFFFFFF…
@PF_296247: pCMV : ATGGCTAGTACGTTAGCTAGCTA : 48.4 : 31 : HEK293mAb-Construct—FFFFFFFFFFF…
@AM_75946: pGEX-4T : ATGCG… : 47.1 : 31 : E colimAb-Construct——
⚠ header field packs 6 values into 1 cell — needs splitting ⚠ "host system" (lowercase + space) ≠ "Host_System" in template ⚠ identifier "+" vs blank — FASTQ quality indicator, not boolean ⚠ No Sample_ID — can't join to other files without parsing header
📅 Without the harmonizer — this is Dr. Priya's week
MON
📂
Opens 6 files. Tries to understand each schema. Takes notes in yet another spreadsheet.
~4 hours
TUE
🗂️
Manually maps each column to the harmonized template. Guesses ambiguous ones like "giprct" and "Agent".
~6 hours
WED
🧹
Fixes "9,6" → 96, "jan 10 24" → 2024-01-10, "2 × 10⁹" → drops it. Re-opens JSON manually.
~5 hours
THU
🔗
Tries to VLOOKUP/merge 6 files on sample IDs. Discovers Sample_Code ≠ Sample_Name ≠ Sample_ID.
~5 hours
FRI
😩
Uploads to Benchling. Schema errors. Fixes manually. Re-uploads. First analysis starts at 4 PM Friday.
~5 more hours
Total: ~25 hours of manual work · No audit trail · Cannot be reused for the next CRO study
✨ With the CRO Data Harmonizer — same Monday, different story
❌ Without Harmonizer
▸Open 6 files, manually read each schema
▸Create mapping spreadsheet by hand (80+ decisions)
▸Fix format errors manually (dates, decimals, units)
▸Attempt VLOOKUP merge — discover key mismatches
▸Upload to Benchling, get schema errors, fix, retry
▸No reusable profile — start from scratch next time
25 hours · 5 days
→
✓ With Harmonizer
▸Upload all 6 files in one drop
▸Claude AI maps all columns with confidence scores + reasoning
▸Review 2 flagged columns — override 1, ignore 1
▸Define one join rule: Sample_Code = Sample_ID
▸Preview 20 rows × 45 columns — looks correct
▸Click "Send to Benchling" → records live in 90 seconds
9 minutes · reusable profile saved

Our Answer

The CRO Data Harmonizer

A full-stack AI platform combining a CRO Data Harmonizer with a Benchling Data Importer — end-to-end, from raw CRO files to live Benchling records.

Claude AI reads your CRO files, understands the data, suggests column mappings with confidence scores and reasoning, and streams records directly into your Benchling tenant.

🤖

Claude AI Mapping Engine

Processes multiple CRO files in parallel, understands scientific context, and provides column mappings with confidence scores and natural-language reasoning.

🔄

Multi-Format Ingestion

Accepts XLSX, XLS, CSV, JSON, TSV, TXT — up to 200MB per file. Multiple CRO source files processed simultaneously.

🏗️

ERD-Driven Architecture

Auto-detects data sections (Sample, DNA, Results, Container) from column names. Maps to Benchling schemas with no mapping file required.

✅

Real-Time Validation & Ingest

Row-level validation, unit checks, duplicate detection, and WebSocket-streamed live ingestion into your Benchling tenant.

Two-Phase Workflow

The Complete Pipeline

Phase 1 — CRO Data Harmonization
01
Upload & Parse
Upload harmonized template + multiple CRO source files (XLSX, CSV, JSON). System parses all files in parallel.
02
AI Column Mapping
Claude AI reads each file, understands scientific context, and suggests column-to-template mappings with confidence scores.
03
Join & Value Rules
Define join rules across CRO files. Apply constant, sequence, or conditional value rules to fill unmapped columns.
04
Preview & Export
Review harmonized dataset (rows × columns). Download as XLSX or send directly to the Benchling Importer.
Phase 2 — Benchling Import (8-Step Wizard)
05
Select Notebook
Choose the destination Benchling folder. Connects live to your tenant via Benchling API.
06
Import Dataset
Upload harmonized file. Auto-detects all data sections and column count. No mapping file required.
07
Schema Selection
Confirm Benchling schema per section. Choose from your tenant's custom schemas or Benchling defaults.
08
AI Mapping → Ingest → Report
AI maps fields at 99% confidence. Review, validate, and stream-ingest records live. Download PDF report.
